enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error/crash) or at minimum flag as invalid any SAM record where sum of query-consuming CIGAR operators (M, I, S, =, X) does not equal length of SEQ field when SEQ != '*'. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop indicates a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: SEQ: segment SEQuence. This field can be a `*' when the sequence is not stored. If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

quarantine.oracle: Parsed header metadata should be identical regardless of intra-line TAG:VALUE pair order

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Parsed reference sequence metadata should be identical regardless of intra-line TAG:VALUE pair order

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Parsed read-group metadata should be identical regardless of intra-line TAG:VALUE pair order

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Parsed program metadata should be identical regardless of intra-line TAG:VALUE pair order

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsed alignment records should be identical regardless of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE field order (columns 12+)

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Parsed alignment position, mapping quality, and query-consuming length must be identical between x and T(x)

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' (ADVISORY) not 'must', so parsers may treat adjacent identical CIGAR ops differently

quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject any SAM record where flag 0x4 (segment unmapped) is set while RNAME != '*' and POS > 0, as this creates a contradictory mapped/unmapped state. The spec states that when 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, etc., implying these fields should be ignored or set to placeholder values.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' for RNAME/* and POS/0 recommendation, but the flag-bit exclusivity is a stronger constraint implied by the 'no assumptions can be made' language.

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag type character violation rejection
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject any SAM record containing an optional tag with a type character outside the allowed set {A, i, f, Z, H, B}. The SAMtags specification defines these as the only valid type characters for optional fields.

evidence.quote: SAM file contents are 7-bit US-ASCII, except for certain field values as individually specified which may contain other Unicode characters encoded in UTF-8.

ambiguity_flags: The exact list of allowed type characters {A, i, f, Z, H, B} comes from the separate SAMtags specification document referenced in the SAM spec, not from the main SAM spec text itself.

quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject or flag as invalid when paired reads of the same template have TLEN values with the same sign (both positive or both negative). The SAM specification requires that for a paired read whose mate is mapped, the two reads of a template MUST carry equal-magnitude opposite-signed TLEN values.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

ambiguity_flags: The exact TLEN sign consistency rule is implied by the paired-read semantics in SAMv1 §1.4 but not explicitly stated as a MUST NOT in the retrieved text chunks. The transform's implementation follows the established convention that TLEN signs must be opposite for paired reads.

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag field ordering invariance for query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each query method where P is the scalar result of the method

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.
Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR operation splitting/merging invariance for query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each query method where P is the scalar result of the method

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' (ADVISORY) not 'must', but splitting/merging identical ops preserves semantic meaning

quarantine.mr_name: SAM 1-based coordinate system invariance under header reordering
quarantine.oracle: The 1-based coordinate system semantics (POS field interpretation) must remain invariant when @SQ header lines are reordered; API query methods returning coordinate-derived properties should produce identical results

evidence.quote: [1-based coordinate system] A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is $[3,7]$. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

1-based coordinate system A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is [3,7]. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

quarantine.mr_name: Header TAG:VALUE pair ordering invariance for query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method where T shuffles intra-line TAG:VALUE pairs in @SQ records

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.
